Definition of 'Use' expressly includes distributing, copying, adapting, modifying as may be technically necessary to use it in a different mode or format.
You are free to: copy, publish, distribute and transmit the Information.
worldwide, royalty-free, perpetual, non-exclusive licence to use the Information with no restriction on private use.
You are encouraged to use and re-use the Information that is available under this licence freely and flexibly.
Exemptions include 'other intellectual property rights, including patents, trade marks, and design rights'.

permissions.commentary: OGL v3.0 does not grant a patent licence. The Exemptions clause explicitly carves patents out of the licensed subject matter; the licence neither grants an express patent right nor forbids the licensor from asserting patents. Patent exposure is therefore out of scope, and default patent law applies.

Default attribution: 'Contains public sector information licensed under the Open Government Licence v3.0.'
